substep.title: Detect Mode
substep.action: Check whether the question tool is available. Available → INTERACTIVE. Absent → AUTONOMOUS. Later substeps branch on this.

check.action:
Confirm topic is present. If absent, emit to stdout and stop:
ERROR: Missing required field 'topic'. The dispatching caller failed to supply complete dispatch context. No study performed.
Confirm the topic is understood and bounded. If genuinely ambiguous, ask one clarifying question via the question tool, recommended interpretation first.

substep.title: Read Mentioned Sources First
substep.action: If the topic names specific files, thoughts/ artifacts, or a prior document, read them FULLY now (no offset/limit) before decomposing. Grounding the study in what the topic points at makes the aspect questions informed rather than guessed.

substep.title: Decompose into Aspects
substep.action: Break the topic into a few (typically 2-4) aspects, each a focused question routed to a child subagent per references/research/child-subagent-dispatch.md. A study of "how our rate limiting works" has aspects: where it lives (locator), how it works (analyzer), what configures it (analyzer), what the edges are (analyzer). Seed aspects with what step 1c established.

substep.title: Single-Domain Gate
substep.action:
A focused study stays within one source domain. If the aspects span domains — codebase AND external best-practice AND prior decisions all needed to answer the topic well — this is a comprehensive investigation, not a focused study. Emit to stdout and stop:
SCOPE_TOO_LARGE Reason: topic spans multiple source domains ({list}) Recommendation: dispatch Comprehensive Investigation, which selects a methodology and synthesizes across domains.
